spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (no direct API hint matched) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (3 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (action, automatically, between, cell, cells, chrome, click, clips, content, editing, ellipsis, every, full, grid, spread).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "When a user interacts (editing or caret navigation) with text which has ‘text-overflow: ellipsis’ set, the text switches temporarily from ellipsis to clip allowing the user to see and interact with th". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("When a user interacts (editing or caret navigation) with text which has ‘text-overflow: ellipsis’ set, the text switches temporarily from ellipsis to clip allowing the user to see and interact with the hidden overflow content. This feature ") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://www.w3.org/TR/css-overflow-3/#ellipsis-interaction to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~2915 chars of script across 3 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
